key_insight: For intermetallic NRR catalysts, s- and p-band descriptors can predict adsorption better than the traditional d-band center, revealing a broader electronic design rule for activity.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, lower-cost screening of non-noble catalyst compositions for sustainable ammonia synthesis using compact ML models trained on DFT data.
method: Built a 47-IMC DFT dataset covering ~1,200 adsorption sites for N2, N2H, and NH3.
Trained machine learning models using intrinsic properties, band centers/fillings, and neighboring-atom Bader charges.
Used SHAP analysis to identify the most important descriptors and rank s/p contributions versus d-band metrics.
evidence_signal: The models achieved MAEs of 0.26 eV for N2, 0.39 eV for N2H, and 0.17 eV for NH3 using only 20 key features.
watch_item: The descriptor ranking is derived from a relatively small chemical space of 47 IMCs, so transferability to broader catalyst families should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: Going Beyond the d-band Center to Design Intermetallic Catalysts for Nitrogen Reduction: A High-Throughput DFT and Machine Learning Study
